verses.text: Amos sees the Lord upon the altar; though they dig to hell or climb to heaven, 'thence will I take them.' No escape. None can flee the reach of the omnipresent God.
Amos sees the Lord upon the altar; though they dig to hell or climb to heaven, 'thence will I take them.' No escape. None can flee the reach of the omnipresent God.
Amos sees the Lord upon the altar; though they dig to hell or climb to heaven, 'thence will I take them.' No escape. None can flee the reach of the omnipresent God.
Even in captivity the sword shall find them; He sets His eyes on them for evil; 'The LORD is his name.' Inescapable justice. The God of all power pursues the guilty everywhere.
Even in captivity the sword shall find them; He sets His eyes on them for evil; 'The LORD is his name.' Inescapable justice. The God of all power pursues the guilty everywhere.
Even in captivity the sword shall find them; He sets His eyes on them for evil; 'The LORD is his name.' Inescapable justice. The God of all power pursues the guilty everywhere.
Are ye not as the Ethiopians to me? He will destroy 'the sinful kingdom,' yet 'not utterly destroy the house of Jacob.' Tempered judgment. Even in wrath God preserves a remnant.
Are ye not as the Ethiopians to me? He will destroy 'the sinful kingdom,' yet 'not utterly destroy the house of Jacob.' Tempered judgment. Even in wrath God preserves a remnant.
He will sift Israel among the nations, 'yet shall not the least grain fall'; the sinners shall die by the sword. Careful sifting. God loses none of His true ones, though He purges the rest.
He will sift Israel among the nations, 'yet shall not the least grain fall'; the sinners shall die by the sword. Careful sifting. God loses none of His true ones, though He purges the rest.
'In that day will I raise up the tabernacle of David that is fallen,' and close its breaches. Davidic hope. James cites this of the gospel gathering of the nations to Christ.
'In that day will I raise up the tabernacle of David that is fallen,' and close its breaches. Davidic hope. James cites this of the gospel gathering of the nations to Christ.

The plowman shall overtake the reaper; God will bring again His people and 'plant them upon their land,' never to be pulled up again. Abundant restoration. The book ends in a promise of secure and lasting blessing.
